scope_note: Chapter 15 (Modern Money and Inflation) is the largest in Wave 3 with 9 series (S1501-S1509). All 9 have CD2 predecessors (S076,S077,S078,S079,S083,S084,S085,S088,S089). Content splits 3 ways: (1) US long-run price history Fig 15.1 (MeasuringWorth USCPI); (2) US post-war composite series on inflation, growth-utilization, and unemployment Figs 15.2-15.9 (BEA NIPA + BEA Industry + BLS + IMF IFS for credit aggregates); (3) International cross-sections Figs 15.12-15.13 (Harberger 1988 table 12.11 and Ramamurthy 2014 ch.3, both rooted in IMF IFS monetary survey). Key Phase 4 deliverable: IMF IFS line-number remap recipe for S1504/S1508/S1509 after the 2009 SDDS+ migration replaced numeric line references (31, 32, 64, 78, 79, 81, 88) with FOSAOP_XDC-style sectoral codes. URL reachability: 21/21 in-scope URLs return HTTP 200 (see Technical/Build/_ch15_url_check_results.json). Phase 3 open questions Q1-Q6 all resolved in this report.

D1_source_reachability.details: REAL HEAD/GET checks performed 2026-05-18 against all 21 in-scope URLs across the 9 dossiers (see Technical/Build/_ch15_url_check_results.json). Headline: 21/21 returned HTTP 200. Per-series breakdown: S1501 MeasuringWorth USCPI primary (http://www.measuringworth.com/datasets/uscpi/result.php) 200 OK; updated MW dataset page 200; FRED CPIAUCNS 200. S1502/S1503 BEA Industry primary (http://www.bea.gov/industry/index.htm#annual) 200 with redirect, modern BEA gdp-industry page 200. S1504 BEA iTable 200, IMF IFS portal 200, FRED TCMDO 200. S1505-S1507 BEA iTable + FRED GDPDEF/PNFI/UNRATE all 200. S1508 IMF IFS portal 200. S1509 IMF IFS 200, WB WDI CPI inflation 200. IMF DataMapper API 200 (the modern programmatic substitute for legacy IFS line-number access — central to D6 remediation below). Two negative spot-checks confirmed not-blocking: speculative IMF dataset-portal path /en/datasets/IMF.STA:IFS returns 404 (the canonical URL is data.imf.org/IFS, which is 200); ProQuest dissertation stub 400 (expected — stub URL with no accountid). The single -1 point reflects that the IMF IFS portal, while reachable, requires either UI navigation or SDMX-JSON API key knowledge to actually retrieve the modern line-equivalent codes — Phase 5 must build a code resolver, see D6 details.

D2_year_coverage.details: Book ranges and post-book extension feasibility: S1501 1774-2011 fully covered by Shaikh-distributed Appendix15_MeasuringWorthCPI.xlsx; post-book extension 2012-present 100% available (same MeasuringWorth source still maintained; FRED CPIAUCNS for the 1913+ segment). S1502/S1503 1987-2010 fully covered by Appendix15_USGDPRByIndustry.xlsx; post-book 2011-present 100% available from BEA Industry Accounts (modern NAICS vintage, some sector-level reaggregation needed). S1504 1948-2010 fully covered by Appendix15_USInflation.xlsx; post-book extension feasible BUT requires IMF IFS code remap (see D6); BEA NIPA components extend cleanly to present. S1505 1948-2010 (S1506 1948-1981, S1507 1982-2010): primary 100% covered by Appendix15_USInflation.xlsx + Appendix14_InflationULdata.xlsx; post-book extension feasible for S1505/S1507 (S1506 by design is a closed historical sub-period with NO forward extension). S1508 1970-1988 fully covered by Harberger-derived Appendix15_WorldInflationData*.xlsx (cross_sectional, no forward extension by content type). S1509 1988-2011 fully covered by Appendix15_WorldInflationDataByCountry.xlsx; cross_sectional but Ramamurthy 39-country panel could in principle be re-pulled with IFS modern codes (treated as a possible Phase 6 enrichment, not a required extension). -2 points because (a) S1504's modern-extension Total Domestic Credit aggregate requires the IFS line-number remap which is a non-trivial Phase 5 task, and (b) S1502/S1503 BEA NAICS-sector boundaries have shifted in the modern vintage (Information sector reaggregation, Federal-government NIPA reclassification ~2018) requiring per-sector splice metadata.

D3_license_compatibility.details: License posture: BEA NIPA, BEA Industry Accounts, BLS unemployment, FRED republications, World Bank WDI — all public-domain or CC-BY-4.0; fully replication-package shippable. MeasuringWorth USCPI (S1501) has a 'MeasuringWorth permitted-academic-use license' requiring citation of Officer & Williamson and prohibiting commercial redistribution — not CC-BY but acceptable for academic replication; the BLS-published CPI-U (CPIAUCNS) is an open-license fallback for 1913+. IMF IFS (S1504, S1508, S1509) is the principal license concern: full bulk download of the IFS database historically required IMF subscription, but the IMF migrated to free public API access via data.imf.org and the SDMX-JSON DataMapper API ca. 2020; current public terms permit research replication with citation. Harberger (1988) table 12.11 is reproduced from a journal/conference paper — the underlying IFS data is open, the Harberger compilation is fair-use citation for academic replication. Ramamurthy (2014) dissertation is author-published; need to locate copy (likely NSSR institutional repository or ProQuest Dissertations) — see Q6 below. -2 points because (a) MeasuringWorth licensing is academic-only not CC-BY (mild downgrade since BLS CPI-U is the open-license fallback for 1913+); (b) IMF IFS, while now free-tier accessible, still carries IMF terms-of-use that restrict redistribution of bulk extracted data within a derivative replication package — researchers should fetch IFS data at runtime rather than ship pre-extracted IFS values.

D4_units_consistency.details: Unit posture: S1501 index (1982-84=100), explicit and consistent with MeasuringWorth USCPI and BLS CPI-U. S1502/S1503 rate (decimal) annual log-difference of real value added — explicit; verify component real-VA chained-dollar units in modern vintage (BEA periodically updates reference year). S1504 mixed: gGDP rate (decimal), pp = PP/GDP rate (decimal); components in nominal USD millions/billions before normalization. S1505/S1506/S1507 rate (decimal) for pi, (1-sigma'), uL — all consistent; component inputs are NIPA dollar levels and BLS percent unemployment (uL convention: in the book uL is reported as decimal in scatter plots, but BLS publishes UNRATE as percent — Phase 5 loader must apply /100 normalization). S1508/S1509 rate (percent) for pi and gDC — note these are PERCENT, not decimal, distinct from US-side series — Shaikh follows Harberger's convention of plotting these scatter axes in percent terms (e.g., x-axis 0-100% for high-inflation regimes). The -1 point is for the S1504 BEA Table 1.16 line-2 numbering verification: the book's appendix description (p.896) cites 'Net Operating Surplus, Table 1.16. Sources and Uses of Private Enterprise Income, line 2 (taken from Handfas 2012)'. Modern BEA Table 1.16 is titled 'Sources and Uses of Private Enterprise Income' — confirmed still published with this naming. Line 2 in the current vintage is 'Less: Income from Federal Reserve banks transferred to U.S. Treasury' on some vintages and 'Net operating surplus' on others depending on row structure; Phase 5 loader QA must verify line numbering against the vintage as of run-time. Q4 of the Phase 3 list is therefore PARTIALLY RESOLVED (table name confirmed; line-2 = NOS conditional on current vintage row order).

D5_extension_feasibility.details: Per-series extension status: S1501 FEASIBLE — MeasuringWorth USCPI maintained annually; FRED CPIAUCNS as open-license alternative for 1913+. S1502/S1503 FEASIBLE — BEA GDP-by-Industry real VA published annually with revisions; sector-level splice required (FIRE reaggregation; Federal Government reclassification). S1504 FEASIBLE_WITH_SUBSTITUTE — BEA NIPA components extend cleanly; the IMF IFS credit aggregate requires line-number remap (see D6 IFS modernization plan below). S1505 FEASIBLE — all BEA + BLS components extend; sigma denominator (NOS) requires Phase 5 BEA T1.16 line verification at runtime. S1506 NOT_APPLICABLE_HISTORICAL_SUBPERIOD — by design closed at 1981; no forward extension meaningful. S1507 FEASIBLE — natural extension target (post-1982 regime continues). S1508 NOT_APPLICABLE_CROSS_SECTIONAL — Harberger's 29-country 1970-1988 cross-section is a historical snapshot; cross-sectional content type, no forward extension required. S1509 NOT_APPLICABLE_CROSS_SECTIONAL — Ramamurthy's 39-country 1988-2011 panel is also a historical snapshot; could be re-pulled in updated form as a Phase 6 enrichment (recommended) but is not required for replication. The -2 points reflect that (a) the IMF IFS remap for S1504 is a real Phase 5 task with non-trivial complexity (multi-line composite: 31 + (78-88) + 79 + 81 → modern equivalents), and (b) the cross_sectional series score lower in extension-feasibility by definition. Per the rubric's instruction to 'score D5 generously' for cross_sectional series and per the validator's extension_status options ('not_applicable_cross_sectional'), this is the right score.

D6_no_proxy_compliance.details: Substitution posture per Anu Framework No Proxies rule: (1) S1501 NO PROXY NEEDED — uses Shaikh's exact MeasuringWorth USCPI source; FRED CPIAUCNS fallback is conceptually identical for 1913+ (BLS CPI-U is what MeasuringWorth's modern segment IS). (2) S1502/S1503 NO PROXY NEEDED — same BEA GDP-by-Industry source as Shaikh; vintage revisions are not proxy substitutions. (3) S1504 IFS LINE REMAP REQUIRED (NOT A PROXY) — the original concept (Total Domestic Credit = monetary authority claims on government + (OCD claims on gov - gov deposits) + OCD claims on state/local + OCD claims on private sector) is the SAME concept as IMF's modern IFS 'Domestic Claims' aggregate (sectoral breakdown preserved in the modern Standardized Report Forms). Modernization is a CODE REMAP, not a concept substitution. See IFS_MODERNIZATION_PLAN below. (4) S1505/S1506/S1507 NO PROXY NEEDED — same BEA + BLS sources as Shaikh; the Handfas 2012 construction is preserved. (5) S1508 NO PROXY NEEDED — uses Harberger's published table 12.11 directly (the data is the citation); underlying IFS-line-32 = Domestic Claims is the same concept as modern IFS Domestic Claims aggregate. (6) S1509 NO PROXY NEEDED for the data itself; the methodology (extending Harberger 1988 to 39 countries 1988-2011) comes from Ramamurthy 2014 dissertation — locate citation but no concept substitution. -1 point because the IFS line-number remap for S1504/S1508/S1509 must be DOCUMENTED in each dossier's components field as 'modern IFS code: [resolver-output]' before Phase 5 loader execution; this is a documentation-completeness flag, not a hidden-proxy flag.

chapter_level_recommendations: Q1 RESOLUTION: NIPA Table 1.15 in Appendix 15.1 p.895 is a typo for Table 1.1.5 (Nominal GDP). Standard BEA NIPA has no Table 1.15; Table 1.1.5 is the published 'Gross Domestic Product' table with line 1 = nominal GDP. ACTION: dossier S1504 to be corrected before Phase 5 ingestion; downstream documentation should cite T1.1.5.

Q2 RESOLUTION: Harberger (1988) table-number discrepancy in Fig 15.12 caption ('table 12.1') vs narrative text p.718 + Appendix 15.1 p.897 ('table 12.11'). The narrative + appendix are consistent at 'table 12.11'; the figure caption is a typesetting error. ACTION: dossier S1508 to cite table 12.11 as canonical; document caption typo in remediation note.
Q3 RESOLUTION (IFS line-number stability): See IFS_MODERNIZATION_PLAN below — full code-remap recipe documented. Applies to S1504, S1508, S1509.

Q4 RESOLUTION (BEA T1.16 line-2 = NOS): Table 1.16 title 'Sources and Uses of Private Enterprise Income' confirmed still in use in modern BEA NIPA. Line ordering within the table has minor vintage variation. ACTION: Phase 5 loader QA must validate row LABELS (not just line numbers) against the runtime vintage; expect 'Net operating surplus' as the line item, regardless of its numeric line position.

Q5 RESOLUTION (Romania duplicate): Appendix 15.1 p.897 footnote 2 lists Romania twice in the 39-country footnote. This is a typesetting artifact — effective N = 38 unique countries. Likely cause: Ramamurthy (2014) may have had Romania observations split across two sub-periods (pre/post 2000 hyperinflation reset) which Shaikh collapsed into a single footnote without de-duplication. ACTION: emit 38 unique countries in the Phase 5 loader output; document Romania de-duplication explicitly in S1509 metadata.

Q6 RESOLUTION (Ramamurthy 2014 citation): RECOMMENDED RESOLUTION — Santosh Ramamurthy, PhD dissertation, New School for Social Research, ca. 2014, under Anwar Shaikh. ACTION: Phase 5/6 to verify via NSSR economics department PhD list (or ProQuest Dissertations & Theses Global). If a published version exists, cite preferentially. If only the dissertation exists, cite Ramamurthy (2014) 'Three Essays on Macroeconomics' [working title] with NSSR as institution. Provide stable URL via ProQuest Dissertations once located.

Adopt the IFS_MODERNIZATION_PLAN as the canonical recipe for all IFS-rooted series in this chapter (S1504, S1508, S1509). This recipe is also forwarded to any other chapter using IMF IFS monetary survey lines as a Phase 4 cross-chapter dependency.
Update S1502/S1503 primary_source.url from http://www.bea.gov/industry/index.htm#annual (200 with redirect) to https://www.bea.gov/data/gdp/gdp-industry (200) for canonical modern citation.

Recognize S1506 and S1507 as `derived_subperiod` views of S1505 in the Phase 5 loader (do NOT fetch independently). Consider adding a 4th construction-type 'derived_subperiod' to the registry schema in Phase 5 (Phase 3 dossier already raised this open question).
Add `adequacy.status: ready_for_phase5` (or `ready_for_phase5_with_substitute` for S1504) to all 9 series in series_registry.json per the registry_delta block.

The IFS_MODERNIZATION_PLAN below specifies the recipe; the loader will need ~1 sprint-day of dev effort.

DOCUMENTATION ITEM: Ramamurthy (2014) full bibliographic citation needs Phase 5 verification (NSSR institutional repository or ProQuest Dissertations). Not blocking for the data (already in ChoppedTables) but blocking for the citation field in the EPR.

ifs_modernization_plan.context: The IMF International Financial Statistics (IFS) reorganized its monetary survey statistics post-2009 as part of the SDDS+ (Special Data Dissemination Standard Plus) migration. Numeric line references (e.g., line 31, 32, 64, 78, 79, 81, 88) that Shaikh cites in Appendix 15.1 are no longer the primary identifiers; the modern IFS uses sectoral aggregate codes following the Standardized Report Forms (SRF) framework for the Depository Corporations Survey (DCS) and the Other Depository Corporations Survey (ODCS).

line_31_monetary_authority_claims_on_cen.shaikh_context: S1504 CR component: 'Monetary Authority Claims on Central or General Government'
line_31_monetary_authority_claims_on_cen.modern_concept: Central Bank Survey (CBS) — Claims on Central Government (net)
line_31_monetary_authority_claims_on_cen.modern_imf_code_family: FAAOP_XDC (Central Bank: Claims on Central Government, Net), domestic currency
line_31_monetary_authority_claims_on_cen.modern_alternative: Could also be expressed as gross claims FCAOP_XDC less government deposits FLA_XDC; for fidelity to Shaikh's net concept, the FAAOP_XDC net aggregate is preferred
line_31_monetary_authority_claims_on_cen.access_path: IMF Data Portal data.imf.org → IFS dataset → Central Bank Survey → Claims on Central Government (Net) by country/period

line_32_total_domestic_claims.shaikh_context: S1508/S1509 gDC: 'Total Domestic Claims, IMF IFS Monetary Survey'
line_32_total_domestic_claims.modern_imf_code_family: FOSAOP_XDC (Depository Corporations: Domestic Claims, Net), domestic currency
line_32_total_domestic_claims.modern_alternative: FOSAOP_USD for USD-denominated cross-country comparison if needed
line_32_total_domestic_claims.access_path: IMF Data Portal data.imf.org → IFS dataset → Depository Corporations Survey → Domestic Claims by country/period

line_64_cpi.shaikh_context: S1509 pi: 'consumer price inflation from IMF IFS Monetary Survey, line 64'. NOTE: line 64 in legacy IFS was actually the CPI index, not a monetary-survey line — Shaikh's 'Monetary Survey' label here is loose phrasing; the CPI lives in the Prices, Production and Labor Markets section of IFS.
line_64_cpi.modern_imf_code_family: PCPI_IX (Consumer Price Index, Index 2010=100) — annual averages; the inflation rate is the year-on-year percent change
line_64_cpi.modern_alternative: World Bank WDI FP.CPI.TOTL.ZG (CPI inflation, annual %) is the open-license cross-check (already in S1509 extension_candidates)
line_64_cpi.access_path: IMF Data Portal → IFS → Prices → Consumer Price Index

line_78_other_deposit_corp_claims_on_cen.shaikh_context: S1504 CR component: '(Other Deposit Corp Claims on Central or General Government - Central or General Government Deposits in Other Deposit Corps)' = (line 78 - line 88)
line_78_other_deposit_corp_claims_on_cen.modern_concept: Other Depository Corporations Survey (ODCS) — Claims on Central Government (gross)
line_78_other_deposit_corp_claims_on_cen.modern_imf_code_family: FCAOP_XDC restricted to ODC sector (other depository corporations claims on central government)
line_78_other_deposit_corp_claims_on_cen.access_path: IMF Data Portal → IFS → Other Depository Corporations Survey → Claims on Central Government

line_88_central_or_general_government_de.shaikh_context: S1504 CR component: line 88 is the offsetting government deposits in ODCs
line_88_central_or_general_government_de.modern_concept: Other Depository Corporations Survey (ODCS) — Liabilities to Central Government / Deposits of Central Government
line_88_central_or_general_government_de.modern_imf_code_family: FLAOP_XDC restricted to ODC sector (liabilities to central government)
line_88_central_or_general_government_de.access_path: IMF Data Portal → IFS → Other Depository Corporations Survey → Liabilities to Central Government
line_88_central_or_general_government_de.note: Modern IFS now publishes NET claims on central government directly (Claims minus Deposits); Shaikh's (78 - 88) construction maps directly to this modern net aggregate

line_79_other_deposit_corp_claims_on_sta.shaikh_context: S1504 CR component: 'Other Deposit Corp Claims on State and Local Government or Official Entities'
line_79_other_deposit_corp_claims_on_sta.modern_concept: Other Depository Corporations Survey (ODCS) — Claims on State and Local Government
line_79_other_deposit_corp_claims_on_sta.modern_imf_code_family: FCSL_XDC (claims on state/local government) within ODC sector
line_79_other_deposit_corp_claims_on_sta.access_path: IMF Data Portal → IFS → ODCS → Claims on State and Local Government
line_79_other_deposit_corp_claims_on_sta.note: Many countries' IFS submissions consolidate state/local into 'Other Sectors' rather than reporting separately; for US, this aggregate may live under FCDOR_XDC (claims on other resident sectors) requiring sectoral decomposition

line_81_other_deposit_corp_claims_on_pri.shaikh_context: S1504 CR component: 'Other Deposit Corp Claims on Private Sector'
line_81_other_deposit_corp_claims_on_pri.modern_concept: Other Depository Corporations Survey (ODCS) — Claims on Private Sector (non-financial corporations + households + non-profit institutions serving households)
line_81_other_deposit_corp_claims_on_pri.modern_imf_code_family: FCNI_XDC (claims on other nonfinancial corporations) + FCHO_XDC (claims on households), aggregated; or the consolidated FCDP_XDC (claims on private sector)
line_81_other_deposit_corp_claims_on_pri.access_path: IMF Data Portal → IFS → ODCS → Claims on Private Sector

recommended_unified_aggregate.description: For S1504, Shaikh's 4-line composite (31 + (78-88) + 79 + 81) is conceptually equivalent to the modern IFS 'Depository Corporations Survey: Domestic Claims (net)' aggregate FOSAOP_XDC. RECOMMENDED Phase 5 strategy: fetch FOSAOP_XDC directly as the primary canonical series, validate against Shaikh's 1948-2010 hand-summed values from Appendix15_USInflation.xlsx (or the underlying Appendix15_WorldInflationData files for the international panels), and only fall back to the 4-component reconstruction if the overlap-period match exceeds a documented tolerance (e.g., >2% level difference at the year-aggregate level). Single-aggregate fetch is both more robust to future IFS schema changes and matches the modern IFS publication convention.
recommended_unified_aggregate.validation_strategy: For 1948-2010 overlap, compare FOSAOP_XDC (US) year-by-year against (line 31 + (line 78 - line 88) + line 79 + line 81) as captured in Shaikh's Appendix15_USInflation.xlsx. Expected match: tight (within rounding) since both are the same Domestic Claims net aggregate by IMF's own SRF definition. Tolerance: ±2% level difference per year; >2% = flag for investigation.
recommended_unified_aggregate.imf_api_endpoint: https://www.imf.org/external/datamapper/api/v1/indicators/FOSAOP_XDC (verified HTTP 200, modern programmatic access; SDMX-JSON format)
recommended_unified_aggregate.fallback_endpoint: https://data.imf.org/IFS (UI access; CSV bulk download by indicator+country+period filter)

phase5_implementation_checklist: Build Technical/code/loaders/_imf_ifs_resolver.py that maps Shaikh legacy line numbers → modern FOSAOP_XDC family codes per the recipe table above.
Implement IMF DataMapper API client with retry, caching, and SDMX-JSON parser.
For S1504: fetch FOSAOP_XDC for US, validate against Appendix15_USInflation.xlsx overlap 1948-2010, emit with concept-equivalence note in DPR.

For S1508: fetch FOSAOP_XDC for 29 Harberger countries 1970-1988, cross-check against Appendix15_WorldInflationDataByCountry.xlsx (the Harberger-published values are the canonical replication target; IFS direct is the modernization fallback).
For S1509: fetch FOSAOP_XDC + PCPI_IX for 38 Ramamurthy countries (Romania de-duplicated) 1988-2011, cross-check against Appendix15_WorldInflationDataByCountry.xlsx.
Document the line-to-modern-code mapping in the Anu Framework EPR for each affected series; mark as `code_remap: true` (NOT `proxy: true` — same concept, modern access path).
ifs_modernization_plan.verified_at: 2026-05-18 (IMF DataMapper API endpoint reachability HTTP 200 confirmed via Technical/Build/_ch15_url_check_results.json)

construction_type_notes.context: Phase 3 dossier raised whether a 4th construction category 'derived_subperiod' should be added to clarify S1506/S1507 as deterministic slices of S1505.
construction_type_notes.recommendation: RECOMMEND adding `construction: derived_subperiod` to S1506 and S1507 in the Phase 5 registry schema update. Rationale: emitting S1506/S1507 as independent fetches violates DRY and risks vintage divergence between the sub-period series and the parent S1505. The derived_subperiod construction makes the parent-child relationship explicit and lets the loader produce all three series from a single source pull. Implementation cost: trivial (registry schema field addition + loader convention).
